question_text: A torch battery stores 12 000 J of chemical energy. The torch is switched on and left running until the battery is completely flat, producing 10 000 J of light energy. Which statement correctly explains what has happened?

options.label: A
options.text: 2000 J of energy has been destroyed because the battery is now empty

options.label: B
options.text: The remaining 2000 J has been transferred to heat energy in the torch and surroundings

options.label: C
options.text: Only 10 000 J of energy ever existed in the battery

options.label: D
options.text: The battery has created light energy from nothing as it ran down

explanation: The law of conservation of energy states that energy cannot be created or destroyed. The 2000 J difference has been converted into heat energy through resistance in the bulb's filament and the battery itself, which is why the torch gets warm during use.
